name: chord-chips-accessible
note: P3-2/P3-5 (P0): chord chips in the practice/chord-sheet header ARE tappable-to-hear (onclick -> packPlayChord) but shipped as bare <span data-c> with no button semantics, no accessible name, and no visible affordance - a first-timer never discovers the app's strongest 'prove it works' moment, and it's invisible to keyboard/screen-reader users. RED-FIRST vs unmodified main: chips render as <span> (not BUTTON, no tabIndex), carry no aria-label, and no .chordHint cue exists anywhere in the practice card.

steps.label: every chord chip is a real, keyboard-focusable control (native <button>, tabIndex >= 0)
every chord chip has a non-empty accessible name that names the chord
steps.js: Array.prototype.every.call(document.querySelectorAll('.chordChips .c'), function(e){ var l = e.getAttribute('aria-label'); return !!l && l.trim().length > 0 && l.indexOf(e.textContent.trim()) >= 0; })
